title: calophyllum wallichianum
intro: Calophyllum wallichianum, also known as the Red Sandalwood or Pterocarpus wightii, is a tropical tree native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Calophyllaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high concentration of calophyllolide, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Calophyllum wallichianum is believed to offer several health benefits, including relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make oils, ointments, and tinctures, which can be applied topically to the skin or used internally as a medicinal supplement.
